My team composition as below. 
Everybody was mentioning about aeqour, however I just joined so totally no clue that water is so strong for clearing story actually.

Afaik, people tend to recommend aequor because tentacle damage and shielding is pretty strong early on and, historically, the aequor banner had a pretty low amount of "bad" awakeners. Issue is, though, that tentacles fall off as you progress since it doesn't scale super well (unless you are running divine aequor, but you do not have any awakeners with it so don't worry about it). Also, nowadays, (almost) all standard awakeners have their niche and all realms have a good balance going on.

My advice would be to check Cheri's guide (particularly, the section "What Banner Should I Pull?") when it comes to pulling advice. As for arc7, you should have no issues running Saya. I'm thinking something like Saya/Thais/Aigis/Clem (Clem dps, try to get her to e2 in this case) or Saya/Thais/Clem/Jenkin (Jenkin dps). You could run GHelot/Thais/Aigis/flex (Clem, Celeste, Faint), but if you are already struggling, then lacking GHelot's enlightments is not helping.
I seeee thank you so so so so much for your detailed analysis !!!

I've been using the new player handbook by Cheri, but it's a little bit overwhelming with the amount of teambuuilding choices there are. Especially with Saya, I have a hard time deciding on anything. I am still very early game, like level 20 characters, so I know it probably won't make much of a difference now, but if I want to have a competitive team later on for endgame are there characters I should invest in? 

From what I remember from before I took a small break, Aigis was a must-have for my teams and I had been running Helot: Catena, Tawil, and Leigh. I can't imagine that's optimal. 

I've played plenty of card games, and have teambuilding experience from other games, it's just a bit hard for me to wrap my mind around all the information I have access to. I've attached a screenshot of all the available character I have.
I also have three "Protoparidise" Awakener selectors, plus one Book of Rotation. I don't know if that makes a difference for any potential teams.

For what content do you need help with teambuilding? Story or dtide? Since, for story, you kinda go and bring the best you can muster in a team (and, therefore, thinking of multiple teams is counter productive), while for dtide the goal is to spread your best units into 5 different teams, since what matters is how well you perform on average.

Considering the state of your account and phrasing, I'll assume you need help with the former, and not the latter. If you need help with dtide, read the corresponding section on Cheri's guide and try to make a couple comps with what you have before asking again.

As for your story comp, GHelot is not going to feel consistent until you get her to e3. If you like how she plays, keep in mind that you'll want to use your prototype horizons on her (check cheri's shop section if you don't know what prototypes are). Her gameplan is setting up her chains and then bursting in one go, so anything that maximizes that 1t burst and helps her survive until that point will be useful. 

While I don't play GHelot (and therefore, take my advice with a grain of salt), I know that Thais is insanely good for her since she dupes her nuke cards and Helot's exalt buffs GHelot's nuke cards by a lot, since they are considered strikes. Aigis is mostly used because 1t petrify helps a lot with surviving and cheesing bosses, besides the vuln debuffs and keyflare generation through playing her 3 cost for free. 

GHelot also benefits a lot from strength sources, which some Ultra units excel at. Clementine is currently on banner and is one of the best at the job, so consider getting her. Horla e1 is also a really really strong support that gives weakness/vuln, strength, aliemus and a really strong damage buff with "anger". Even if you decide not to play with GHelot, these two will always be a good investment.

As for more survivability, some more options that come to mind are Celeste and Faint.

Honestly, what I would do in your position is to try and get Thais and e1 Horla through the Moonless Guide banner (if you have yet access to it, check that section in Cheri's guide) and aim for something like GHelot/Thais/Aigis/flex (Horla e1, Clem, Leigh). If that comp felt like it was too fragile, then I would swap the flex spot for something like Faint or Celeste. In the meantime, I would run GHelot/Leigh/Aigis/Celeste.

Again, take my team comp advice with a grain of salt, since I'm sure I'm missing something from not being a GHelot player. Also, if you don't have a predisposition towards GHelot, consider checking Saya comps instead. Saya is currently on banner too (she likes her e1 a lot), is an insanely strong support and has multiple comp options, but has anti synergy with GHelot. You can check more about her here: https://discordapp.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1514650465458065459
Thank you for the advice! This makes a lot of sense. I'm not going to really worry too much about the eventual endgame because I'm still very early, and this puts some things into perspective.
